message:
fix(parser): correct word boundary detection for SQL keyword matching

Fix find_keyword_at_depth_zero() to treat underscores as word characters
when detecting SQL keyword boundaries.

## Problem (br-22iss)
The word boundary check only tested for alphanumeric characters:
  let before_ok = !upper.as_bytes()[i - 1].is_ascii_alphanumeric();

This incorrectly matched keywords embedded after underscores, e.g.,
matching "FROM" in "SELECT_FROM" or within identifiers containing
underscore-prefixed keywords.

## Solution
Add is_word_char() helper that treats both alphanumerics AND underscores
as word characters:
  let is_word_char = |b: u8| b.is_ascii_alphanumeric() || b == b'_';

Now "SELECT_FROM" won't match "FROM" because '_' precedes it.

## Tests added
- infer_columns_table_qualified_with_alias: verifies table-qualified
  column parsing with AS aliases (m.id, m.subject, a.name as from_name)
- infer_columns_lowercase_as: verifies lowercase 'as' keyword handling

These test patterns match real queries from mcp-agent-mail-db.

## Additional tests (br-22iss regression coverage)
- update_with_numbered_placeholders_in_where: UPDATE with 3 numbered
  placeholders (?1, ?2, ?3) in SET and WHERE clauses
- update_with_four_numbered_placeholders_in_where: exact E2E scenario
  with 4 placeholders (policy = ?1, timestamp = ?2 WHERE project = ?3
  AND name = ?4)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
